posts.author: StockSutra
posts.text: A "fundamentally good" company usually does four things well. Here's how I judge each with free sources. — Completely fair — and we won’t pretend otherwise. There are a lot of tools being pushed here, and the fatigue is real. We’d rather earn a place than claim one. The stock market isn’t just numbers on a screen — it’s understanding a business, the risks around it, and the constant tug-of-war between sentiment and fundamentals. That’s not something a quick pitch can prove; it’s something we have to show over time. So we’ll do less promoting and more helping. If there’s ever a stock you’re try…

posts.author: StockSutra
posts.text: A "fundamentally good" company usually does four things well. Here's how I judge each with free sources. — Hi, Both fair points — and honestly, you’re partly right. A good prompt to ChatGPT or Claude can give you a solid overview, and if you enjoy doing that, you genuinely should. Let me be straight about where a tool like this is actually different, and where it isn’t. The biggest gap isn’t the writing — these models write well. It’s the numbers. When you ask ChatGPT for “fundamental analysis of [company],” it often works from old or incomplete figures, and when something’s missing it can fil…

posts.author: ANKIT_T
posts.text: A "fundamentally good" company usually does four things well. Here's how I judge each with free sources. — A more easier way. Give a prompt to to Chat GPT/Gemini saying 'Fundamental Analysis of [Company Name]. It will tell you all the pros and cons. Then take a call based on that. You can also ask follow up questions to clarify your doubt, if any. And all this is absolutely FREE

posts.author: Sankalp
posts.text: A "fundamentally good" company usually does four things well. Here's how I judge each with free sources. — ₹3,000/month feels a bit steep. If I’m already paying for Claude, with good prompting and the right data sources, I feel I could get very similar insights. The pricing would make more sense if there were clearly differentiated proprietary data or workflows that aren’t easily replicable.

posts.author: StockSutra
posts.text: A "fundamentally good" company usually does four things well. Here's how I judge each with free sources. — It earns more every year, and the cash is real. Pull 5 years of revenue and profit (Screener.in). You want a steady climb, not one lucky year — then sanity-check it against cash flow from operations . If profit rises but cash doesn’t, the earnings may be more accounting than reality. It isn’t living on borrowed money. Check debt against equity, and whether profits comfortably cover the interest. A business buried in debt can be wiped out by a single bad year. The people running it are inv…
